title: Top-level src dry-run cannot reach ManalinkEh.dll build
evidence: (cd src && make -n) exits 2 at functions/utility.obj; src/card_id.h is absent while Program/src/card_id.h is tracked.
why it matters: Top-level source tree cannot currently prove a rebuild of the patched runtime DLL.
suggested fix: Document Program/src as the more complete snapshot; add a safe generated-header restoration plan before attempting builds.
test strategy: dry-run make again after header/build-rule cleanup; compare produced DLL hashes only in a throwaway output folder.

title: Windows-oriented build tools are unavailable locally
evidence: yasm, dlltool, objcopy, and windres are absent; makefiles call them.
why it matters: DLL/resource builds cannot complete on this machine without a toolchain.
suggested fix: Document exact MinGW/yasm/binutils prerequisites and keep build outputs outside runtime folders.
test strategy: Re-run tool availability and make dry-runs after installing tools in a controlled environment.

title: src and Program/src are divergent source snapshots
evidence: diff -qr reports many differing source files; only Program/src/card_id.h exists.
why it matters: It is unclear which source snapshot corresponds to shipped DLLs, so source-only fixes may not affect runtime.
suggested fix: Create a source-map/provenance matrix before any source-to-binary claim.
test strategy: Compare build outputs from both trees in throwaway directories if build blockers are resolved.

title: Build helper mutates source and copies DLLs to hardcoded c:\magic2k paths
evidence: src/build.pl rewrites ManalinkEh.asm, runs make, then copies ManalinkEh.dll to c:\magic2k and c:\magic2k\zips.
why it matters: Running it casually can mutate source and external installs.
suggested fix: Add --dry-run/--out options and refuse to copy unless explicitly requested.
test strategy: Run perl syntax check and dry-run mode in a throwaway copy.

title: Deploy batch performs destructive deletes/copies under c:\magic2k
evidence: src/deploy.bat starts with del /s/q c:\magic2k\zips\magic\src and many hardcoded copy commands.
why it matters: It is a historical packaging script, not safe repo-local automation.
suggested fix: Document as historical; do not run except in a prepared Windows packaging copy.
test strategy: No runtime test; static review only.

title: CardArtLib Cards.dat loader has unchecked file/allocation/read paths
evidence: loadDat() does not check fopen/fread/malloc before reading Cards.dat and indexing strings.
why it matters: Bad or missing Cards.dat can crash the process before useful error reporting.
suggested fix: Add checks for file open, read counts, allocation, and string bounds in source; do not patch DLL without build proof.
test strategy: Unit-style loader test against malformed copy of Cards.dat after build support exists.

title: CardArtLib path formatting can overflow fixed buffer
evidence: idToNameFun() sprintfs CardArtManalink/%s (%d).jpg into char tmp[254] using card names from data.
why it matters: Long names or corrupted data can overwrite stack memory.
suggested fix: Use snprintf and validate id/name lookup.
test strategy: Build/test CardArtLib against long synthetic name data in a throwaway harness.

title: Drawcardlib frame-name formatting can overflow static buffer
evidence: get_pichandlename() sprintfs base_dir/config names into static char buf[3][160].
why it matters: Long base paths or config names can overflow; path length is plausible with deep installs.
suggested fix: Use snprintf and increase/derive buffer size.
test strategy: Build/test with long install path/config names.

title: Drawcardlib config key concatenation can overflow fixed buffer
evidence: get_cfg_int2_raw() strcpy/strcat key1+key2 into char buf[104].
why it matters: New or malformed config keys can overflow.
suggested fix: Use snprintf or length-checked concatenation.
test strategy: Build/test config parser with long keys.

title: Draft custom-set parser uses unbounded fscanf into fixed buffers
evidence: load_custom_set() reads %[^\n] and %[0-9] into fixed buffers without width limits and does not check fopen results.
why it matters: Malformed DraftSets files can crash or corrupt memory.
suggested fix: Add width specifiers, fopen checks, and line-based parsing.
test strategy: Test with malformed DraftSets copies after build support exists.

title: Draft mode writes generated files into runtime working directory
evidence: load_custom_set and draft helpers write draft_errors.txt, picks.txt, packs.txt, and Playdeck files by relative path.
why it matters: Wrong working directory can dirty runtime roots or fail silently.
suggested fix: Document CWD requirements and consider a configured output directory in source.
test strategy: Manual draft-mode test in disposable copy.

title: Program/Shandalar.exe layout is missing adjacent zlib.dll in MTG bottle
evidence: Existing docs record direct C:\Shandalar\Program\Shandalar.exe failing because Program\zlib.dll is absent.
why it matters: Users can launch the wrong path and hit loader failures.
suggested fix: Keep root Shandalar as primary; test Program layout only in a copied install after DLL layout decision.
test strategy: CrossOver/native visible launch from exact Program path after adding/copying dependencies in a disposable copy.

title: Root and Program Magic.exe differ but are both launch-relevant
evidence: Docs and hashes show different SHA-256 values; root Shandalar opens root Magic.exe while launcher uses Program/Magic.exe.
why it matters: Patching/testing only one copy leaves behavior inconsistent.
suggested fix: Always document exact path/hash and keep patch verification for both copies.
test strategy: Separate launch and hash verification for both Magic.exe paths.

title: Root and Program ManalinkEh.dll differ by hash and patch offset
evidence: Healer patch sites are 0x3bb035 and 0x381a25 with different SHA-256s.
why it matters: Copying the wrong DLL beside a Magic.exe path can miss or mismatch runtime fixes.
suggested fix: Use path-specific hash/offset verification before copying to any install.
test strategy: xxd/shasum both adjacent DLLs and copied install DLLs.

title: Declared-attacker undo may not reverse attack costs or attack-choice triggers
evidence: Patch clears STATE_ATTACKING before Done; docs note attack costs/triggers can run before/around selection.
why it matters: Undo may be safe for ordinary attackers but wrong for unusual costs/triggers.
suggested fix: Keep patch documented as conservative and require manual cases for costs, triggers, vigilance, and banding.
test strategy: Manual gameplay checklist R3/R4 plus edge-case attacker tests.

title: Damage-prevention freeze patch may not cover other prevention handlers
evidence: Current fix gates Samite/Femeref/Kithkin shared handler; docs note other GAA_DAMAGE_PREVENTION handlers may exist.
why it matters: Similar off-window activation freezes may remain for other cards.
suggested fix: Inventory all GAA_DAMAGE_PREVENTION handlers before more binary patches.
test strategy: Manual repro for other prevention cards; source/static handler inventory.

title: Unimplemented DDBM/subtype flags assert instead of graceful fallback
evidence: Regex/TODO search finds ASSERT lines for unimplemented damage_dealt_* and subtype_deals_damage modes.
why it matters: If a new card path reaches these flags, the game may abort instead of failing gracefully.
suggested fix: Keep in backlog; convert to recoverable behavior only with card regression tests.
test strategy: Targeted scenario tests for each flag before any source/runtime change.

title: GDI object/DC lifetime is hand-managed in many UI paths
evidence: GDI search finds repeated CreateCompatibleDC/Bitmap, SelectObject, DeleteObject, DeleteDC, ReleaseDC patterns.
why it matters: Leaks or invalid selected-object deletion can destabilize long sessions.
suggested fix: Audit functions one at a time and add restore/delete conventions in source.
test strategy: Static review plus long-session GDI handle monitoring on Windows.

title: Manalink launcher mutates local Program/Mods trees
evidence: Launcher deletes temp folders, renames sound/stat folders, installs mods, and deletes draft decks relative to %~dp0.
why it matters: It is useful but can change runtime assets; package-root choice matters.
suggested fix: Run only in a test copy for cleanup/install-root investigations.
test strategy: Disposable-copy launch/menu test; compare file tree before/after.

title: Optional static analysis coverage is missing
evidence: cppcheck, scan-build, clang-tidy, infer, and semgrep are not installed.
why it matters: Current audit is regex/static reading, not semantic analysis.
suggested fix: Add optional analyzer docs or CI after build prerequisites are settled.
test strategy: Re-run tools when installed; store reports under docs/generated/code-audit.
